name: Mexican-Style Breakfast Casserole
instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Generously spray a small baking dish with cooking spray to prevent sticking.

2. Spray a small pan with cooking spray and heat over medium heat. Add the diced Onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until the onion has softened and turned translucent, about 5 minutes.

3. Transfer the cooked onions to a large bowl and mix in the Frozen Hash Browns, diced Ham, some Mexican Cheese Blend, and diced Green Chile Peppers. Spread this mixture evenly in the bottom of the prepared baking dish, creating a hearty base layer.

4. In a separate bowl, whisk together the Eggs, Milk, Sour Cream, and Taco Seasoning until smooth and well combined. Pour this custard mixture evenly over the hash brown layer, allowing it to seep into all the gaps.

5. Bake in the preheated oven until the casserole is set and a knife inserted into the center comes out clean, 45 to 60 minutes. Check every 5 minutes toward the end to avoid overbaking.

6. Remove from the oven and immediately spread the remaining Mexican Cheese Blend evenly on top. Return to the oven and bake just until the cheese is melted and bubbly, about 3 minutes.

7. Let the casserole stand for 5 minutes to set and make slicing easier before serving hot.
simpleInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven and spray the baking dish with cooking spray.

2. Cook the diced Onion until soft and translucent.

3. Mix the onions with Frozen Hash Browns, diced Ham, some cheese, and diced chiles, then layer in the dish.

4. Whisk the Eggs, Milk, Sour Cream, and Taco Seasoning together and pour over the hash brown mixture.

5. Bake until set, then add the remaining Mexican Cheese Blend on top and bake briefly to melt.

6. Let stand before slicing and serving.
